title: Here’s what happens if you inject the Masseters incorrectly 😱 #medicalaesthetics #botox #botoxnatura
filename: Here’s what happens if you inject the Masseters incorrectly 😱 #medicalaesthetics #botox #botoxnatura.en.vtt
excerpt: For anyone that's ever had Botox I need you to tell me if this is normal or if this has ever happened to you or like will it go away my face is frozen and I tried to smile and bro so there are three reasons why Botox could cause a smile side effect and this particular case if you…

segments.text: for anyone that's ever had Botox I need you to tell me if this is normal or if this has ever happened to you or like will it go away my face is frozen and I tried to smile and bro so there are three reasons why Botox could cause a smile side effect and this particular case if you treat orbicular succuli it's an accessory muscle that meant it's meant to help pull your cheek up a little bit I don't think that's what's causing it with this patient though the next reason is if you inject too deeply and you go through orbicular circular underneath that is the origin of the zygomaticus major which runs down and pulls the mouth up like this and that is also one of the core muscles with smiling but I don't think that's actually what's caused it with her I think the most likely cause is the master reduction that she had has affected the rosarius it seems to be quite a common side effect although I very rarely I've never had it with one of my patients I've seen a number of cases now this muscle pulls it's attached to the master so if you shrink the size of the mess that you might

affect this muscle here that pulls sideways so you end up with a smaller smile that's what's happened it's avoidable I have other videos on it check them out on my page but you can definitely avoid the side effect if you inject Cafe

transcript: for anyone that's ever had Botox I need you to tell me if this is normal or if this has ever happened to you or like will it go away my face is frozen and I tried to smile and bro so there are three reasons why Botox could cause a smile side effect and this particular case if you treat orbicular succuli it's an accessory muscle that meant it's meant to help pull your cheek up a little bit I don't think that's what's causing it with this patient though the next reason is if you inject too deeply and you go through orbicular circular underneath that is the origin of the zygomaticus major which runs down and pulls the mouth up like this and that is also one of the core muscles with smiling but I don't think that's actually what's caused it with her I think the most likely cause is the master reduction that she had has affected the rosarius it seems to be quite a common side effect although I very rarely I've never had it with one of my patients I've seen a number of cases now this muscle pulls it's attached to the master so if you shrink the size of the mess that you might affect this muscle here that pulls sideways so you end up with a smaller smile that's what's happened it's avoidable I have other videos on it check them out on my page but you can definitely avoid the side effect if you inject Cafe
